title: Route from Cairo to Jordan — Sep 2023 trip planning
html:
TL;DR

  • Sep 5–8 2023 outbound to Alexandria, Egypt → bus down to cairo → flight to Aqaba (gateway to petra) → up to amman → return via Cairo → Seattle.
  • $476 one-way Seattle → Alexandria — Yahir's standard non negotiables for traveling entry-point (cheap, asymmetric, multi-leg).
  • Three-day decision window: tickets had to be bought within the next 3 days from doc creation, revealing the trip's last-minute commitment energy.
  • Mid-doc audible: pivoted to "fly to Jordan first, then fly back to US from Alexandria" as the more natural path.
  • 12-day total trip: 1-2 days Alexandria → 3-4 days Cairo → 4 days Jordan (petra / amman / Aqaba) → 1 day return through Cairo → fly home.

The trip shape

The route as Yahir wrote it:

  • Day 1: Fly Seattle → Alexandria
  • Days 1-2: Land, alexandria egypt
  • Days 3-6: Bus to cairo
  • Days 7-10: Fly to Aqaba (or petra direct), explore jordan — petra, amman, Aqaba
  • Day 11: Return to cairo
  • Day 12: Fly Cairo → Seattle

Three sub-routes considered for the Jordan leg:

  1. Aqaba → bus to Petra → bus to Amman
  2. Direct flight to Petra, then Amman
  3. Fly Amman → bus to Petra → bus back to Amman

Mid-doc Yahir audibles: "Complete audible.. Fly to Jordan, then flying back to the US from Alexandria. That is another more natural path." Optimization-on-the-fly.

The prep checklist

Yahir's standard pre-trip diligence (which appears compressed in his earlier 2021 07 29 LIMA CUSCO origin-trip doc and expanded here):

  • Local Uber availability per city
  • Hostel network research, alternative sleeping situations
  • Average daily spend
  • Proper attire (especially relevant for Jordan/Egypt cultural norms)
  • Bank notification of out-of-country travel
  • Cell-service strategy: probably rely on WiFi + WhatsApp

Why it matters in the graph

This is the second formal travel-planning artifact in the vault after 2021 07 29 LIMA CUSCO (Peru) — and it shows the non negotiables for traveling pattern hardening:

  • Cheap entry flight ($476 one-way to Alexandria, vs. $528 round-trip to Lima two years earlier)
  • Multi-country in one swing (Egypt + Jordan)
  • Buffer-day return through entry city
  • Last-minute commitment ("buy tickets within the next 3 days")
  • Hostel network + flexible-sleep-location

The fact that Yahir audibled mid-doc rather than committing to the pre-written route is also characteristic — the plan exists to be revised once new information surfaces.

This trip also lands petra on his itinerary — petra is one of the New Wonders of the World on his lifetime list (per 2024 07 02 Alt Summer Plan referencing his "wonders" tracker). Combined with machu picchu (done in 2021) and taj mahal (planned for 2024), this 2023 Jordan trip would have been wonder #2 in the count.

Privacy

No credentials in this doc.
